headline: Nord Anglia opens Sixth Form Centre in Hung Hom with triple-pathway offer
dek: Nord Anglia International School Hong Kong will launch a purpose-built Sixth Form Centre in August 2026, making it the first international school in the city to offer A Levels, the IB Diploma and BTEC under one roof.
author: marcus-chen

body.text: Nord Anglia International School Hong Kong (NAIS Hong Kong) will open a brand-new Sixth Form Centre in Hung Hom in August 2026, marking what the school describes as a milestone in its continued growth. The centre, located at 11 Wan Hoi Street, adds a dedicated fourth campus to the school's existing Early Years, Primary and Secondary sites across Sai Kung, Lam Tin and Kwun Tong.

The facility is designed to offer students A Levels, the International Baccalaureate Diploma Programme and BTEC pathways — a combination that, according to the school, makes NAIS Hong Kong the first international school in the city to offer all three post-16 qualifications simultaneously. The A Level curriculum will sit alongside the existing IB Diploma route, giving students aged 16 to 18 the option to specialise in three or four subjects rather than the broader IB framework.

The Hung Hom centre is purpose-built to mirror a university-style environment. It includes specialist science laboratories, a modern library, dedicated study and collaborative spaces, a fitness area and a flexible creative space for performance, projects and exhibitions. The school has said the design philosophy centres on fostering independence and preparing students for the transition to higher education.

To mark the launch, NAIS Hong Kong has introduced a Sixth Form Scholarship Programme open to both current students and external applicants. Admissions for the inaugural 2026–27 cohort are already open, with the school noting that places are limited for Year 12 entry.
Kellett School also joining Hong Kong's A Level revival

NAIS Hong Kong is not alone in expanding its sixth form provision. Kellett School — Hong Kong's longstanding British international school — is simultaneously opening a new Sixth Form Centre at The Bay Hub on Kai Cheung Road, opposite its existing Kowloon Bay campus, also targeting August 2026 and subject to Education Bureau approval. The Kellett centre is designed to accommodate up to 240 students and will serve Years 11 and 12.

The parallel announcements signal what school advisers describe as a timely revival of A Levels in Hong Kong, where the IB Diploma has long dominated the post-16 international school landscape. For families weighing subject specialisation against the breadth of the IB, the arrival of credible A Level alternatives at established British-curriculum schools broadens the choice considerably ahead of the 2026–27 academic year.

NAIS Hong Kong is part of the Nord Anglia Education family, which operates more than 80 schools across 30 countries. Students at the Hung Hom centre will retain access to the group's Global Campus platform and its institutional collaborations with MIT and the Juilliard School.
